report_name: AD CS Escalation Deep Dive: How Attackers Abuse Certificate Templates for Domain Dominance

threat_actors.name: Fighting Ursa
threat_actors.description: A state-sponsored actor cited as an example of groups exploiting Active Directory Certificate Services (AD CS) for privilege escalation and domain control.

attack_patterns.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
attack_patterns.description: The core purpose of abusing AD CS is to escalate from a low-privilege account to a high-privilege one, such as a domain administrator.

attack_patterns.name: Steal or Forge Authentication Certificates
attack_patterns.description: Attackers exploit misconfigured certificate templates (e.g., ESC1 technique) to request certificates with a Subject Alternative Name (SAN) of a privileged user, effectively forging an authentication certificate to impersonate them.

attack_patterns.name: Domain Account
attack_patterns.description: Attackers enumerate domain accounts to identify high-privilege targets, such as domain administrators, for impersonation via malicious certificate requests.

attack_patterns.name: Steal or Forge Kerberos Tickets
attack_patterns.description: After obtaining a malicious certificate for a privileged user, attackers use tools like Rubeus to request a Kerberos Ticket-Granting Ticket (TGT) for the impersonated account, granting them domain-wide access.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The ultimate goal of the AD CS exploitation is to gain access to and use valid, high-privilege accounts, such as domain administrator accounts, for lateral movement, persistence, and achieving objectives.

attack_patterns.name: Account Manipulation
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use a persistence technique known as shadow credentials, where they modify an attribute on a user or computer object (like msDS-KeyCredentialLink) to add their own certificate-based credential, allowing them to authenticate as that user without their password.

malware.name: update6.exe
malware.description: A file named update6.exe was observed in an attempt to exploit CVE-2022-26923 for privilege escalation.

tools.name: Certipy
tools.description: Used by attackers to enumerate vulnerable certificate templates in Active Directory Certificate Services and to request malicious certificates for privilege escalation.

tools.name: Rubeus
tools.description: Used by attackers to request a Kerberos Ticket-Granting Ticket (TGT) using a certificate obtained from AD CS, allowing impersonation of a privileged user.

tools.name: Certify
tools.description: Mentioned as a tool used for AD CS enumeration, similar to Certipy.

tools.name: certreq.exe
tools.description: A legitimate Windows utility that can be used to request certificates, and is monitored as a potential indicator of malicious activity.

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2022-26923
vulnerabilities.description: A vulnerability in Active Directory Certificate Services that could be exploited by attackers to elevate privileges by acquiring a malicious certificate.

indicators.name: AD CS Exploitation File update6.exe
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: AD CS Exploitation Attempt File Creation
id: 7c5a3d1e-9b8f-4c6d-8a7b-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a file named 'update6.exe', which has been associated with attempts to exploit CVE-2022-26923 for privilege escalation.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/active-directory-certificate-services-exploitation/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1068
  - attack.privilege_escalation
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\update6.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the file `update6.exe` which was associated with attempts to exploit CVE-2022-26923.

indicators.name: Certipy Vulnerable Template Enumeration
indicators.pattern:
title: Certipy Vulnerable AD CS Template Enumeration
id: 8d9e0f1a-2b3c-4d5e-8f9a-0b1c2d3e4f5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the Certipy tool to find vulnerable Active Directory Certificate Services templates.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/active-directory-certificate-services-exploitation/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1087.002
  - attack.discovery
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\certipy.exe'
      - '\certipy'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'find'
      - '-vulnerable'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects command-line execution of the Certipy tool to find vulnerable AD CS certificate templates.

indicators.name: Certipy Malicious Certificate Request
indicators.pattern:
title: Certipy Malicious AD CS Certificate Request
id: 9a0b1c2d-3e4f-5a6b-7c8d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of the Certipy tool to request a certificate, potentially for privilege escalation.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/active-directory-certificate-services-exploitation/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1649
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\certipy.exe'
      - '\certipy'
    CommandLine|contains: 'req'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects command-line execution of the Certipy tool to request a certificate from AD CS.

indicators.name: Suspicious Access to AD CS Web Enrollment Page
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Access to AD CS Web Enrollment Page
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-a7b8-c9d0-e1f2a3b4c5d6
status: experimental
description: Detects network traffic to AD CS web enrollment pages from unusual client workstations, which could indicate reconnaissance or exploitation attempts.
references:
  - https://unit42.paloaltonetworks.com/active-directory-certificate-services-exploitation/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1649
  - attack.credential_access
level: low
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri-stem|contains: '/certsrv/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects network traffic to the AD CS web enrollment pages (e.g., /certsrv/), which could indicate reconnaissance or an attempt to request a certificate.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious Access to AD CS Web Enrollment Page
